design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect items, combine them, use inventory solutions on environment and NPCs to solve puzzles and progress through narrative
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Uncovering the corruption at the heart of Union City while navigating a dystopian cyberpunk world
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as a wilderness-raised outsider infiltrating and exposing a totalitarian city-state's secrets
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate tonal balance between serious cyberpunk themes and humour-filled dialogue; rejects both Sierra's earnestness and LucasArts's slapstick in favour of a middle ground. Features protagonist death as a consequence mechanic (unusual for adventure games of the era), establishing failure as possible rather than prevented.

difficulty.checkpointing: Player-controlled save points; protagonist death triggers reload from last save
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Remastered version adds hint system and hotspot highlighting, granting players direct difficulty adjustment control

progression.upgrade_system: Joey (sentient robot companion) can change physical bodies/shells as situations require; circuit board personality persists across form changes
progression.unlock_mechanism: Item collection and puzzle solving gates progression; dialogue trees with NPCs provide clues and unlock information
replayability_hooks: Dialogue trees with NPCs offer different information pathways
Hint system in remaster may encourage different solution approaches

world_building_techniques: Comic book prologue establishes Robert Foster's backstory and world context
NPC dialogue trees reveal plot, puzzles, and world lore incrementally
Environmental storytelling through clickable objects in the world
Political world-building through NPC conversations about Unions vs Corporations, economic war with Hobart

faction_indicators: Union City (neo-democratic state controlling labour and removing social benefits)
Hobart Corporation (rival city-state engaged in economic sabotage)
LINC (the all-powerful computer intelligence governing Union City)
Aboriginal tribe that raised Robert Foster (outsider society)
danger_signals: Security soldiers sent to kidnap Robert and annihilate his tribe
Helicopter malfunction upon arrival in Union City suggests internal conflict
LINC's unexplained abduction of Robert indicates surveillance state control
environmental_storytelling: The Gap (Outback wasteland) represents civilisation's environmental damage
Union City architecture and design reflect dystopian cyberpunk setting
Contrast between wilderness survival (Robert's past) and urban corruption (Union City present)
